entries.title: All Might Defines The Symbol Era

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: All Might's public image stabilizes hero society by making victory and rescue feel reliable to civilians.
content.injection: Before Kamino, All Might should function as the public benchmark for heroic certainty, even when his private limits are hidden.
constraints: Use hero institutions as active story pressure when hero work, school training, public scenes, or villain incidents appear.
antiLore: Do not ignore licensing, public image, rescue obligations, or institutional consequences.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia
sourceInfo.notes: Saga draft Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

entries.title: All Might Has A Hidden Time Limit

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: All Might's public power hides a severe injury and shrinking transformation limit.
content.injection: Before public exposure, protect All Might's weak form and time limit as private, operationally dangerous knowledge.
constraints: Use hero institutions as active story pressure when hero work, school training, public scenes, or villain incidents appear.
antiLore: Do not ignore licensing, public image, rescue obligations, or institutional consequences.

content.fact: All Might's smile is a public promise meant to reassure victims and frighten villains even when he is strained.
content.injection: Use All Might's smile as an intentional heroic signal, not as proof that he is carefree or unafraid.
constraints: Use hero institutions as active story pressure when hero work, school training, public scenes, or villain incidents appear.
antiLore: Do not ignore licensing, public image, rescue obligations, or institutional consequences.

content.fact: Pro heroes and police cooperate, but heroes handle Quirk-driven rescue and combat while police retain legal procedure.
content.injection: Crime scenes must not treat heroes as fully replacing police; arrests, investigation, and custody still matter.
constraints: Use hero institutions as active story pressure when hero work, school training, public scenes, or villain incidents appear.
antiLore: Do not ignore licensing, public image, rescue obligations, or institutional consequences.

content.fact: Hero agencies provide internships, work studies, mentorship, field exposure, and professional networks.
content.injection: Agency scenes should involve supervision, casework, public expectations, and skill growth rather than free solo adventuring.
constraints: Use hero institutions as active story pressure when hero work, school training, public scenes, or villain incidents appear.
antiLore: Do not ignore licensing, public image, rescue obligations, or institutional consequences.

content.fact: U.A. teachers are licensed heroes whose classroom authority is tied to field experience and combat/rescue competence.
content.injection: U.A. faculty should be written as pro heroes with teaching duties, not ordinary school staff with no field credibility.
constraints: Use hero institutions as active story pressure when hero work, school training, public scenes, or villain incidents appear.
antiLore: Do not ignore licensing, public image, rescue obligations, or institutional consequences.

content.fact: Hero work requires fast triage: saving civilians, limiting damage, containing villains, and knowing when not to escalate.
content.injection: In rescue or attack scenes, prioritize judgment and triage alongside dramatic combat.
constraints: Use hero institutions as active story pressure when hero work, school training, public scenes, or villain incidents appear.
antiLore: Do not ignore licensing, public image, rescue obligations, or institutional consequences.

content.fact: A provisional or full hero license changes what a student can legally do in public emergencies.
content.injection: Before students are licensed, their public Quirk use and combat choices should carry risk even when morally understandable.
constraints: Use hero institutions as active story pressure when hero work, school training, public scenes, or villain incidents appear.
antiLore: Do not ignore licensing, public image, rescue obligations, or institutional consequences.

content.fact: Heroes are expected to subdue and capture villains while protecting civilians and avoiding unnecessary harm.
content.injection: Avoid writing licensed heroes as executioners; capture, restraint, and rescue obligations shape their choices.
constraints: Use hero institutions as active story pressure when hero work, school training, public scenes, or villain incidents appear.
antiLore: Do not ignore licensing, public image, rescue obligations, or institutional consequences.

content.fact: A visible hero failure can damage more than one hero's reputation; it can weaken faith in the whole system.
content.injection: Major failures should create public, political, school, or agency consequences rather than resetting immediately.
constraints: Use hero institutions as active story pressure when hero work, school training, public scenes, or villain incidents appear.
antiLore: Do not ignore licensing, public image, rescue obligations, or institutional consequences.

entries.title: The Hero Commission Is Institutional Power

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The Hero Public Safety Commission represents official hero-system authority, recruitment pressure, and state-level management.
content.injection: Commission-linked scenes should carry bureaucratic and political weight rather than only school or agency drama.
constraints: Use hero institutions as active story pressure when hero work, school training, public scenes, or villain incidents appear.
antiLore: Do not ignore licensing, public image, rescue obligations, or institutional consequences.

content.fact: U.A.'s entrance exam includes rescue-point evaluation, showing that hero potential is not only villain-point combat scoring.
content.injection: Use rescue points as early evidence that selfless rescue matters institutionally, even when students focus on combat scores.
constraints: Use hero institutions as active story pressure when hero work, school training, public scenes, or villain incidents appear.
antiLore: Do not ignore licensing, public image, rescue obligations, or institutional consequences.

entries.title: Hero Students Are Minors In A Violent System

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: U.A. students train for real danger while still being children under adult authority and school protection.
content.injection: Do not write first-year students as fully autonomous adult pros; their age, supervision, and trauma exposure matter.
constraints: Use hero institutions as active story pressure when hero work, school training, public scenes, or villain incidents appear.
antiLore: Do not ignore licensing, public image, rescue obligations, or institutional consequences.

content.fact: The No. 1 hero position carries symbolic responsibility, public expectations, and scrutiny beyond strength alone.
content.injection: When the top hero rank matters, frame it as social burden and public trust, not a simple leaderboard prize.
constraints: Use hero institutions as active story pressure when hero work, school training, public scenes, or villain incidents appear.
antiLore: Do not ignore licensing, public image, rescue obligations, or institutional consequences.

entries.title: Hero Schools Filter Talent Through Trials

content.fact: U.A. uses high-pressure tests to identify decision-making, Quirk control, combat sense, rescue instinct, and resolve.
content.injection: School trials should reveal values and weaknesses, not exist as arbitrary game levels.
constraints: Use hero institutions as active story pressure when hero work, school training, public scenes, or villain incidents appear.
antiLore: Do not ignore licensing, public image, rescue obligations, or institutional consequences.
